_meta.description: Per-fiscal-year COMPONENTS for the Industry Snapshot ring cards (segment-mix + PSU-vs-private). Three sourced figures per year: industry total LIFE premium (IRDAI), LIC total premium (= public-sector life), and the four PSU general insurers' premium (= public-sector general). The card derives the rest live: private life = life_total − LIC; private GI = total_gi − public_gi (total_gi comes live from industry-segment-premium.json, GI Council, swept every 3 days). PSU = LIC + public-GI; Private = everything else. Public sector follows the STANDARD definition: LIC + New India + National + Oriental + United India. The two state-owned specialised insurers (AIC crop, ECGC export-credit) are NOT counted as public.
_meta.refresh_cadence: annual. Each year add ONE row with that year's three components and the card advances on its own (the GI total is already live). Sources by component: life_total_premium — IRDAI Annual Report (~Dec); lic_total_premium — LIC full-year results press release (~May); public_gi_premium — GI Council / IRDAI year-end public-sector general figure. Until ALL three are sourced for a year, the card stays on the last fully-sourced year — missing is never zero, never fabricated.
_meta.metric_basis: total premium (first-year + renewal), Rs crore — a premium metric, not profit
_meta.notes: Replaces the earlier BEST-ESTIMATE composition, which wrongly applied LIC's ~57% NEW-BUSINESS (first-year) share to TOTAL premium. LIC's share of TOTAL life premium is ~55% (488148 / 886000), not 57%. Now built from published figures: LIC's own total-premium disclosure and the government-confirmed public-sector general premium (four PSU general insurers, ~Rs 1.06 lakh cr in FY25). Resulting FY25 split: Private 50.2% (Rs 5,99,513 cr) vs PSU 49.8% (Rs 5,94,148 cr); the totals now reconcile exactly with the segment-mix card. FY26 STATUS (as of 2026-06): LIC's FY26 total premium is published (Rs 5,35,984 cr) and staged below; the whole-industry FY26 life total on a TOTAL-premium basis is NOT out yet (only the new-business figure ~Rs 4.59 lakh cr is), and the exact FY26 public-sector general figure isn't published yet either — so those stay null and the card correctly holds at FY25 until they land (missing ≠ zero).

data.basis: FY26 in progress. LIC total premium published; industry life total (total-premium basis) and the four PSU general insurers' FY26 figure not yet published — kept null, not estimated.

provenance.source_name: LIC FY26 results (year ended 31-Mar-2026): total premium income Rs 5,35,984 cr, +9.80% YoY. Industry life total and public-sector general FY26 pending publication (Life Council / IRDAI / GI Council).

data.basis: Total premium (first-year + renewal), Rs crore. Public sector = LIC + the four PSU general insurers (New India, National, Oriental, United India). Private = all private life insurers + private general insurers + standalone health insurers. Specialised public insurers (AIC, ECGC) excluded from public, per the standard definition.

provenance.source_name: IRDAI Annual Report 2024-25 (industry total life premium, Rs 8.86 lakh cr); LIC FY25 results press release 27-May-2025 (LIC total premium income Rs 4,88,148 cr); GI Council / IRDAI FY25 (public-sector general premium ~Rs 1.06 lakh cr — four PSU general insurers, per the 29-May-2025 PSGIC review)
